subject: Updating the supportedPolicyTypes in config files
commit_message:
Updating the supportedPolicyTypes in config files

Changing the supportedPolicyTypes in apex-pdp configuration files
to "onap.policies.controlloop.operational.Apex"

Change-Id: Ie92d5284fbd80da48318ce7863471876ccb380bb
Issue-ID: POLICY-1632
Signed-off-by: a.sreekumar <ajith.sreekumar@est.tech>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/policy-apex-pdp-master-verify-java/698/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/policy-apex-pdp-master-verify-java/698

Patch Set 1:

I thought the design for both apex and pdp is t 
 work with the "legacy" type : "onap.policies.controlloop.Operational" which will be evolved next release.   Distinction would be based on how should handle it, apex, or pdp-d would be based on the group.   Has this changed or needs to change for some reason?

Patch Set 1:

Another consideration if there would be upstream changes needed on the api component rest apis for policy creation .. ?

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

Need some feedback on this and if there are implications to other components, api, pdp-d and how it plays with policy creation, see API : main/src/main/java/org/onap/policy/api/main/rest/LegacyApiRestController.java

Patch Set 1:

> I thought the design for both apex and pdp is t
 > work with the "legacy" type : "onap.policies.controlloop.Operational"
 > which will be evolved next release.   Distinction would be based on
 > how should handle it, apex, or pdp-d would be based on the group.  
 > Has this changed or needs to change for some reason?

Yes, we discussed it in today's scrum. If apex & drools has same policy type then the problem is with the simple deploy. PAP will try to check that we have atleast one instance of Pdp assigned to the each subgroup and will attempt to deploy the legacy operational policy to both apex & drools. In that case, apex will fail to deploy the policy as the grammar for writing apex/drools policy is different. As a result of that PAP will then unregister apex instance.
Also, this was the reason of simple deploy failing in the Pairwise testing on last Friday.
Keeping the policy type separate will help in resolving the interference between apex & drools. 
Any legacy operational policy will be tagged as "onap.policies.controlloop.Operational" and only attempted in drools.
For Apex, user will create ToscaPolicyType, ToscaPolicy, assign it to subgroup and deploy policies using PdpGroup deploy API. And for default preconfigured subgroup we will have "onap.policies.controlloop.operational.Apex".

Patch Set 1:

> Another consideration if there would be upstream changes needed on
 > the api component rest apis for policy creation .. ?

No changes in API component.
Changes for PAP DB Initialize is already up for review.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

> Need some feedback on this and if there are implications to other
 > components, api, pdp-d and how it plays with policy creation, see
 > API : main/src/main/java/org/onap/policy/api/main/rest/LegacyApiRestController.java

Legacy Policies are only targeted towards drools.

Patch Set 1:

I think this is ok. Going forward we'd like control loop designers to be able to select operational.Drools vs operational.Apex policies. So if this helps get us through testing in Dublin that is good. We will address when we work on the feature to remove legacy operational policies.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

Thanks for the feedback folks
Change has been successfully merged by Liam Fallon
